title: [DebugInfo][InstCombine] When replacing bswap idiom, add DebugLoc to new insts

target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

!2 = !DILocation(line: 5, column: 3, scope: !0)

define i16 @bswap_i16(i16 %x) !dbg !2 {
entry:
  %and1 = and i16 %x, 255
  %shl1 = shl i16 %and1, 8
  %and2 = and i16 %x, 65280
  %shr2 = lshr i16 %and2, 8
  %result = or i16 %shl1, %shr2, !dbg !2
  ret i16 %result, !dbg !2
}

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary focuses on i64 bswap patterns from the inflate example, but i16 byte-swap is a different integer width not explicitly covered. The debug location propagation applies equally but tests narrower width handling.

tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-i128:128-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

!0 = distinct !DICompileUnit(language: DW_LANG_C, file: !1)
!1 = !DIFile(filename: "test.c", directory: "/")
!2 = !DILocation(line: 10, column: 5, scope: !0)

define i32 @bswap_phi(i32 %cond, i32 %a, i32 %b) !dbg !2 {
entry:
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %cond, 0
  br i1 %cmp, label %if, label %else, !dbg !2

if:
  %and1 = and i32 %a, 255
  %shl1 = shl i32 %and1, 24
  %and2 = and i32 %a, 65280
  %shl2 = shl i32 %and2, 8
  %and3 = and i32 %a, 16711680
  %shr3 = lshr i32 %and3, 8
  %and4 = and i32 %a, 4278190080
  %shr4 = lshr i32 %and4, 24
  %or1 = or i32 %shl1, %shl2, !dbg !2
  %or2 = or i32 %or1, %shr3, !dbg !2
  %bswap_a = or i32 %or2, %shr4, !dbg !2
  br label %merge, !dbg !2

else:
  %bswap_b = call i32 @llvm.bswap.i32(i32 %b), !dbg !2
  br label %merge, !dbg !2

merge:
  %phi = phi i32 [ %bswap_a, %if ], [ %bswap_b, %else ], !dbg !2
  ret i32 %phi, !dbg !2
}

if:

  br label %merge, !dbg !2

else:

merge:

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary describes simple load/store bswap patterns, but this test involves bswap decomposition within control flow with PHI nodes. Debug location propagation through branching contexts is not explicitly addressed.

define i64 @bswap_interleaved(i64 %x, i64 %y) !dbg !2 {
entry:
  %and1 = and i64 %x, 255
  %add_temp = add i64 %y, 1, !dbg !3
  %shl1 = shl i64 %and1, 56
  %and2 = and i64 %x, 65280
  %mul_temp = mul i64 %add_temp, 2, !dbg !3
  %shl2 = shl i64 %and2, 40
  %and3 = and i64 %x, 16711680
  %shr3 = lshr i64 %and3, 24
  %and4 = and i64 %x, 4278190080
  %shr4 = lshr i64 %and4, 8
  %and5 = and i64 %x, 1095216660480
  %shr5 = lshr i64 %and5, 8
  %and6 = and i64 %x, 280375465082880
  %shr6 = lshr i64 %and6, 24
  %and7 = and i64 %x, 71776119061217280
  %shr7 = lshr i64 %and7, 40
  %and8 = and i64 %x, 18374686479671623680
  %shr8 = lshr i64 %and8, 56
  %or1 = or i64 %shl1, %shl2, !dbg !2
  %or2 = or i64 %or1, %shr3, !dbg !2
  %or3 = or i64 %or2, %shr4, !dbg !2
  %or4 = or i64 %or3, %shr5, !dbg !2
  %or5 = or i64 %or4, %shr6, !dbg !2
  %or6 = or i64 %or5, %shr7, !dbg !2
  %result = or i64 %or6, %shr8, !dbg !2
  ret i64 %result, !dbg !2
}

define i64 @bswap_interleaved(i64 %x, i64 %y) !dbg !2 {
entry:
  %add_temp = add i64 %y, 1, !dbg !3
  %mul_temp = mul i64 %add_temp, 2, !dbg !3

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary shows clean bswap patterns, but this test has other operations (add, mul) interleaved with the bswap decomposition sequence. Debug loc propagation through mixed operation sequences is not explicitly covered.
